body:
This PRD was selected from the durable 2026-07-18 Unbidden Oath author-playtest report and ratified at the subsequent `/to-prd` testing-seam checkpoint. On 2026-07-18, the user ratified this single intended PRD, the bounded 500-character author-focus rule, mounted-session lifecycle, exact request-to-prompt behavior, inspected-fingerprint send gate, existing core/server/component testing seams, and publication with `enhancement` plus `ready-for-agent`. The durable originating evidence is `reports/playtest-the-unbidden-oath-2026-07-18T145754Z.md`; the local PRD-prep determination was summarized, not cited, because it is not published on the repository publication ref. The relevant active authorities are `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md`, `docs/compiler-contract.md`, `docs/ideation-prompt-template.md`, `docs/user-guide.md`, and `docs/ACTIVE-DOCS.md`.

## Problem Statement

Ideate can deterministically inspect the selected continuity, current Generation Brief, requested mode, requested count, dormant-slot choice, and current slate avoidance input, but it has no declared channel for the author's actual uncertainty. An author who needs ideas about a specific local problem therefore receives a grounded slate that may still be irrelevant to the decision they are trying to make.

The workaround is to rewrite the Generation Brief solely to steer Ideate or to accept generic ideas and mentally translate them back to the real question. Rewriting canonical or prompt-facing continuity for a temporary brainstorming need blurs authority, while generic retries spend provider calls without repairing the missing request context.

The current send path also does not prove that the prompt the author inspected is the focused prompt rebuilt for the provider request. Adding author focus without an inspected-fingerprint gate would let rapid edits or stale UI state send a different request from the one visibly reviewed.

## Solution

Add one optional Author focus control to Ideate, labeled in author language as “What do you need ideas or questions about?” The value is trimmed, limited to 500 characters, held only for the mounted Ideate session, and visibly identified as non-canonical request context. Blank focus preserves today's generic grounded Ideate behavior.

When nonblank, the exact normalized focus appears once in the inspected ideation prompt, inside the existing request header of `<ideation_slots>`. It changes the prompt fingerprint and asks the model to shape responses within the already assigned grounded slots. It does not select records, create new grounds, change operator eligibility, prevent intentional count shrinkage, or grant authority over continuity.

Editing focus recompiles the local preview. The existing Get ideas, Get new slate, Regenerate all, and per-slot Regenerate actions remain the only provider-send actions. Each send carries the fingerprint of the visibly inspected prompt; the server rebuilds the full focused request and fails before provider transport if the fingerprint is stale or the focus is invalid.

## User Stories

1. As an author, I want to state the local uncertainty I need Ideate to explore, so that grounded suggestions address my actual decision
2. As an author, I want the focus control to ask a plain-language question, so that I understand what belongs there without learning an internal prompt term
3. As an author, I want focus to be optional, so that I can continue using today's generic grounded ideation workflow
4. As an author, I want blank or whitespace-only focus to preserve the current generic prompt behavior, so that adding the control does not change unfocused Ideate requests
5. As an author, I want a visible character count, so that I can stay within the bounded request size while writing
6. As an author, I want an accessible over-limit error, so that I know why preview or send is unavailable and how to recover
7. As a keyboard or assistive-technology user, I want the focus label, count, help, and error to be programmatically associated, so that I can understand and correct the field without relying on visual placement
8. As an author, I want shortening an over-limit focus to recompile the prompt locally, so that recovery does not require a provider call or page reload
9. As an author, I want the exact normalized focus visible in Prompt Inspector, so that I can verify what the external model will receive
10. As an author, I want focus to appear exactly once in the prompt, so that the request is inspectable without accidental weighting through duplication
11. As an author, I want focus labeled as non-canonical request context in both the control and prompt, so that a temporary question cannot be mistaken for story state
12. As an author, I want focus edits to change the prompt fingerprint, so that prompt freshness reflects the request I actually inspected
13. As an author, I want rapid focus edits to mark older previews stale, so that an out-of-order compile response cannot silently restore obsolete request text
14. As an author, I want sending disabled until the displayed preview matches current focus, so that I cannot knowingly send an uninspected focused prompt
15. As an author, I want the server to rebuild and fingerprint-check the focused prompt, so that stale browser state fails closed before provider transport
16. As an author, I want an over-limit direct request rejected by the server, so that bypassing the browser cannot send invalid focus to the provider
17. As an author, I want typing and preview compilation to remain local, so that merely formulating a question does not contact OpenRouter
18. As an author, I want only the existing idea and regeneration actions to send focused context, so that the external-model boundary remains explicit and pull-based
19. As an author, I want focus preserved when I switch between Ideas and Questions, so that changing output form does not erase my question
20. As an author, I want focus preserved when I change Count or Dormant slot, so that tuning the slate does not erase my question
21. As an author, I want focus preserved through full-slate regeneration, so that I can request another answer to the same uncertainty
22. As an author, I want focus preserved through per-slot regeneration, so that replacing one weak result keeps the same authorial aim
23. As an author, I want Clear all to clear only slate scratch and keepers, so that discarding model output does not discard my still-relevant question
24. As an author, I want focus reset when the Ideate view is remounted, so that a temporary question does not leak into a future ideation session
25. As an author, I want focus excluded from project storage, browser storage, backup, migration, export, and accepted-segment provenance, so that it remains temporary request context
26. As an author, I want Ideate to read no Private Note data into focus, so that author-private scratch remains outside every prompt source
27. As an author, I want candidates, accepted prose, rejected responses, and assistance scratch excluded from focus automatically, so that prose and model output cannot silently become a new prompt source
28. As an author, I want focused Ideate output to remain quarantined scratch, so that relevance does not grant ideas continuity authority
29. As an author, I want focus to leave slot operators and grounding unchanged for identical records and other request inputs, so that it shapes responses without becoming hidden source selection
30. As an author, I want intentional slate shrinkage preserved, so that focus cannot pad unsupported slots with invented material
31. As an author, I want focus and prompt text excluded from logs, so that temporary creative questions do not create a hidden prompt archive
32. As an implementer, I want one normalized focus value shared by preview and send, so that client and server do not develop competing request contracts
33. As an implementer, I want existing ideation routes and test surfaces extended rather than duplicated, so that the feature remains inside the current deep module boundaries
34. As a maintainer, I want the prompt contract, active template, user guidance, versions, and golden evidence updated together, so that implementation cannot drift from the documented behavior

## Implementation Decisions

1. **One stable request field.** The ideation request gains one optional string field named `focus`. This is a synthesized interface default selected to carry one unambiguous value through preview, send, inspection, and tests; it does not create a second focus authority or a parallel request object.

2. **One shared normalization and limit.** Focus is trimmed at the request boundary. Missing, empty, and whitespace-only values normalize to blank. A nonblank normalized value may contain at most 500 characters under one shared count rule used by the core contract, server validation, browser count, and tests. The 500-character bound was explicitly ratified at the seam checkpoint.

3. **Byte-compatible blank behavior.** Blank focus adds no prompt line and preserves the current unfocused ideation prompt, fingerprint, slot assignment, request defaults, and generic output contract byte-for-byte. This default preserves existing behavior rather than turning the new optional input into mandatory prompt noise.

4. **Reuse the existing request header.** A nonblank focus renders exactly once in the request header of `<ideation_slots>`, after the mode declaration and before assignment details, as `Author focus (non-canonical request context): <normalized value>`. The current request-level mode, slate size, and assigned slots already meet in this section, so placing focus there avoids a second prompt section or a new source seam.

5. **Treat focus as escaped data.** Prompt rendering escapes focus with the compiler's existing data-text rules so focus cannot break section boundaries. The prompt tells the model to use it only to shape responses within assigned slots and never as story fact, continuity authority, a new named entity, or permission to contradict compiled records.

6. **No slot-selection effect.** Focus is excluded from citation assignment, operator eligibility, grounding-bundle choice, dormant-record selection, count shrinkage, record ordering, and output parsing. For identical records and all other request fields, every focus value produces the same slot assignment; only the request line and resulting fingerprint differ.

7. **Mounted-session lifecycle.** Focus is ordinary Ideate view state. It survives mode, Count, Dormant slot, Get new slate, Regenerate all, per-slot Regenerate, and Clear all while the view remains mounted. It resets to blank when the view remounts and is never copied into keeper storage.

8. **Clear all remains scratch-only.** Clear all continues to remove the current slate and keepers. It does not reset focus or the other request controls. This preserves the existing meaning of clearing model output rather than broadening it into a form reset.

9. **No durable or hidden source.** Focus is not project data, story configuration, a story record, a Generation Brief field, active-working-set membership, candidate data, accepted prose, assistance output, browser storage, backup data, migration input, export data, or provenance metadata. No control imports Private Notes, candidates, accepted segments, prior prompts, or assistance scratch into focus.

10. **Preview freshness is explicit.** Any focus edit immediately makes the displayed preview ineligible for send and starts local recompilation. Only the latest successful compile for the current normalized request may restore preview-ready state. Older out-of-order compile responses must not replace the current preview.

11. **Inspected-fingerprint send contract.** Every `/api/ideate` send carries the fingerprint returned by the currently displayed ideation preview. The server rebuilds the prompt from current project state and the complete ideation request, compares the rebuilt fingerprint with the inspected fingerprint, and returns the existing fail-closed stale-prompt outcome before provider transport when they differ. This reuses the repository's established Generate and Segment Reconciliation fingerprint pattern.

12. **Layered invalid-input rejection.** The browser shows the character count and accessible error, withholds send while focus is invalid, and recovers after correction. Core/server request validation independently rejects over-limit or malformed focus before provider transport. No invalid-input path invokes OpenRouter.

13. **Existing provider boundary only.** Typing, validation, normalization, preview compilation, and Prompt Inspector updates are localhost-only operations. Get ideas, Get new slate, Regenerate all, and per-slot Regenerate remain the only user actions that may make one OpenRouter request. No automatic retry, background send, or provider fallback is added.

14. **Regeneration carries one current request.** Whole-slate and per-slot regeneration send the current normalized focus together with the current mode, Count, Dormant slot, existing avoid-list behavior, and inspected fingerprint. A regeneration cannot silently fall back to blank or reuse an earlier focus.

15. **One behavior revision and one prerequisite spec.** Because this changes an LLM-assistance request, deterministic prompt compilation, prompt fingerprinting, inspection, and browser send behavior, implementation begins with one active spec under the repository's change-intake rules. The same behavior revision updates `docs/compiler-contract.md`, `docs/ideation-prompt-template.md`, `docs/user-guide.md`, the relevant entries in `docs/ACTIVE-DOCS.md`, prompt/compiler/contract versions, frozen ideation prompt evidence, and implementation tests.

16. **No constitutional amendment.** `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md` already permits explicit deterministic assistance-request inputs under the prose-aligned Ideate profile. The implementation must preserve its source exclusions, pull-based provider boundary, prompt inspection, deterministic compilation, output quarantine, and no-automatic-canon rules without amending them.

17. **Output lifecycle is unchanged.** Focus affects only the inspected request and the external model's bounded relevance instruction. Parsed ideas, malformed raw output, keepers, Clear all, and the absence of insert/apply/use-as-prose actions keep their current quarantine and persistence behavior.

### Browser-visible guidance checklist mapping

| Checklist item | PRD home |
|---|---|
| `entry point and availability` | Solution and Implementation Decisions keep Ideate as the entry point, make focus optional whenever ideation preview is available, and withhold send while current focus lacks a matching preview |
| `user-visible states, actions, and outcomes` | User Stories and Implementation Decisions cover blank, filled, over-limit, compiling, preview-ready, stale, sending, regenerated, cleared, and remounted states across every existing Ideate action |
| `validation, warning, error, and recovery behavior` | User Stories, Implementation Decisions, and Testing Decisions cover the visible count, accessible over-limit error, correction and recompile recovery, stale-fingerprint failure, and zero-transport guarantees |
| `prompt preview contents and freshness` | Solution and Implementation Decisions require the exact normalized focus once in `<ideation_slots>`, prompt fingerprint changes, latest-request preview ownership, and inspected-fingerprint send verification |
| `user-initiated external LLM boundary` | Solution and Implementation Decisions state that typing and preview stay local and only Get ideas, Get new slate, Regenerate all, and per-slot Regenerate may send one request |
| `canon and prose boundary visibility` | Solution, User Stories, and Implementation Decisions label focus as non-canonical request context and forbid automatic record, brief, note, candidate, accepted-prose, or assistance-output sourcing |
| `persistence, migration, export, and provenance` | User Stories and Implementation Decisions make focus mounted-session state only and explicitly exclude project storage, browser storage, backup, migration, export, and accepted-segment provenance changes |
| `browser and accessibility regression scenario` | Testing Decisions reuse the Ideate component seam for the labeled textarea, described count/help/error, keyboard operation, preview freshness, send gating, retention, Clear all, and remount reset |

## Testing Decisions

Tests assert externally observable request, prompt, route, provider-boundary, and browser behavior rather than private helper structure. Prior art is the core package's ideation request-rendering and frozen-prompt tests, the server package's ideation compile/send route and end-to-end tests, the existing stale-fingerprint route tests for provider-bound workflows, and the web package's Ideate component tests.

Seam confirmation: answered on 2026-07-18 with the existing core ideation request/compiler and golden-prompt seam, server ideation compile/send route seam, and Ideate component seam. No new test-only interface, browser framework, full browser journey, or external-model call is introduced.

1. **Core ideation request/compiler seam.** Cover trimming, blank normalization, the shared 500-character boundary, escaped data text, exact one-line placement, byte-compatible blank behavior, deterministic recompilation, changed fingerprint for changed nonblank focus, unchanged slot assignment across focus values, unchanged intentional count shrinkage, and the frozen ideation prompt baseline.

2. **Server ideation compile/send route seam.** Cover focused preview output, send-time rebuild with the complete request, matching fingerprint success under an instrumented transport, stale fingerprint rejection, missing fingerprint rejection, over-limit and malformed request rejection, and zero transport calls for every failure. Retain the existing no-project, readiness-blocked, missing-key, malformed-output, citation, determinism, secret-leakage, and no-persistence behaviors.

3. **Ideate component seam.** Cover the accessible label, help, live count, error association, blank and filled states, local compile-on-edit, out-of-order compile protection, send gating until the latest preview is ready, exact prompt readback, provider action boundaries, focus retention through every control and regeneration action, Clear all preserving focus while clearing slate/keepers, and blank reset after remount.

4. **Documentation and spec conformance.** Review the active spec and authority updates against `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md` Section 29, the exact request shape and prompt destination in `docs/compiler-contract.md` and `docs/ideation-prompt-template.md`, the user-facing lifecycle in `docs/user-guide.md`, active-doc registration, and version/golden consistency.

5. **Canonical gates.** Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, and `npm run build`.

## Principles

- `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md` Sections 4.1, 4.4, 6.4, 6.6, 8, 9.1, 22, 23, 26.1, 27, and 29 govern this feature. The PRD preserves author-owned continuity, deterministic compilation, declared assistance-request input, Private Notes exclusion, prompt inspection, API-key secrecy, pull-based provider invocation, and quarantined assistance output.
- `docs/compiler-contract.md` remains the deterministic bridge for the new request field, prompt destination, empty behavior, escaping, fingerprint effect, exclusions, and version change.
- `docs/ideation-prompt-template.md` remains the domain authority for the Ideate request shape, section order, grounding rules, output contract, and UI quarantine.
- `docs/user-guide.md` must describe the optional Author focus control, local preview behavior, explicit send boundary, session-only reset, and non-canonical status in author language.
- `docs/ACTIVE-DOCS.md` governs creation and registration of the required active spec and keeps authority routing current.
- The Section 29 alignment checklist was applied. The feature adds no branches, plot rails, automatic record changes, accepted-prose source, Private Notes source, hidden record selection, nondeterministic compiler input, prompt archive, provider-secret exposure, background assistance, or remote data authority.
- No relevant ADR exists in the current repository, no `docs/FOUNDATIONS.md` amendment is required, and no deliberate exception is taken from an active authority.

## Out of Scope

- Persisting focus in project data, browser storage, settings, story configuration, records, the Generation Brief, active-working-set state, backup, migration, export, or accepted-segment provenance
- Importing, linking, prefilling, or deriving focus from Private Notes, story records, candidates, accepted prose, rejected or superseded output, prior prompts, keepers, or any other assistance scratch
- Changing selected records, working-set membership, citation keys, operator taxonomy, slot eligibility, grounding-bundle selection, dormant-slot rules, requested-count shrinkage, output parsing, citation verification, or malformed-output quarantine
- Changing the existing avoid-list contract except to carry it alongside the same current focus and inspected fingerprint during regeneration
- Promising model relevance, completeness, creativity, count compliance, or response quality beyond putting the inspected bounded request in the deterministic prompt
- Automatically inserting ideas into records, the Generation Brief, candidates, prose prompts, or accepted output
- Adding automatic retry, background send, provider fallback, a second provider request, model-specific prompt forks, or provider-specific hidden transforms
- Changing OpenRouter settings, model refresh, candidate acceptance, Accepted Segments, Record Hygiene, Segment Reconciliation, Generation Brief semantics, Records, navigation, Story Configuration, or Prompt Search behavior
- Bundling the report's narrow ticket and verification follow-ups into this PRD

## Further Notes

Seam confirmation: answered on 2026-07-18 with the existing core ideation request/compiler and golden-prompt seam, server ideation compile/send route seam, and Ideate component seam. The user also ratified the single intended package, bounded request, session-only lifecycle, exact prompt placement, fingerprint gate, and `enhancement` plus `ready-for-agent` publication posture.

The exact-title duplicate guard found no existing issue with this PRD title. The `enhancement` and `ready-for-agent` labels were verified in the current tracker, and every browser-visible guidance checklist item has a concrete home above. No product, mechanism, testing seam, label, or publication-package decision remains open to veto.

This is not a multi-PRD program and has no PRD-sized predecessor or deferred PRD candidate. The source report's separate Non-PRD Follow-Up items remain outside this publication: Reconciliation No-Change Trust Warning, Voice Pressure Cast Picker, Private Note Single-Identity Verification, Desktop Navigation Reachability, Cast Creation Prerequisite Guidance, Prompt Search Match Navigation, Story Configuration Save-State Consistency, and Cast Record Scanability. They remain ticket or coverage candidates and are not created, sequenced, or ratified by this issue.

labels.name: needs-triage
labels.description: Maintainer needs to evaluate this issue

title: PRD: Author-Focused Ideation — Bounded Non-Canonical Request Context
